task_completion.description: Verify that Stellarium is set to Griffith Observatory and the correct date/time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window shows 'Griffith Observatory' or coordinates ~34.118 N, 118.300 W. The Date/Time window shows 2023-06-04 and 21:00:00 UTC (or equivalent local time).

task_completion.description: Verify that the required display settings are enabled in Stellarium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view shows a bright blue daytime sky (atmosphere enabled), curved grid lines (azimuthal grid enabled), and text labels for planets and stars are visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that Venus is located and centered in the Stellarium view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Venus is selected and centered in the Stellarium viewport, with the daytime sky and azimuthal grid visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that Jupiter is located and centered in the Stellarium view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jupiter is selected and centered in the Stellarium viewport, with the daytime sky and azimuthal grid visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that Sirius is located and centered in the Stellarium view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Sirius is selected and centered in the Stellarium viewport, with the daytime sky and azimuthal grid visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the outreach plan text file is created with the required content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the contents of 'daytime_outreach_plan.txt' on the Desktop, containing the words 'Griffith', 'June', 'Venus', 'Jupiter', and 'Sirius'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Stellarium application window open with visible menus, toolbars, or search dialogs being used to configure the scene.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshots of the targets are genuine Stellarium captures.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots of Venus, Jupiter, and Sirius show the Stellarium interface or viewport rendering, not web browser images or stock photos.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually created the text file rather than just viewing the prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or terminal command (e.g., echo, cat) is visible actively writing or displaying the created file at ~/Desktop/daytime_outreach_plan.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires Stellarium to be configured for Griffith Observatory (34.118° N, 118.300° W) on 2023-06-04 at 21:00:00 UTC. The display must show the atmosphere (blue sky), azimuthal grid, star names, and planet hints. The agent must capture screenshots of Venus, Jupiter, and Sirius. The text file at ~/Desktop/daytime_outreach_plan.txt must contain the keywords: griffith, june, venus, jupiter, sirius.
